_purpose: Map Fibonacci growth to ontology entities. How LTs scale, recur, and deepen across spirals.

spirals.storyDepth: single scene, present tense, one character speaks
spirals.note: Maximum repetition. Each game introduces 1 new LT. The student hears, selects, names.

spirals.storyDepth: multi-scene, simple past introduced, two characters interact
spirals.note: 5 of 8 games revisit A1 LTs in new contexts (new ecosystem, new characters). 3 games introduce A2-specific targets (preterite, reflexives, imperative). Same form, richer situation.

spirals.storyDepth: narrated past, cause-effect, allies and enemies, community conflict
spirals.note: 8 of 13 games recycle A1+A2 targets with the TIME dimension. 'El río es frío' (A1) becomes 'El río era frío cuando Yaguará llegó' (B1). Same LT, new tense, same ecosystem. 5 games introduce B1-specific targets (preterite vs imperfect, subjunctive basic, por/para).

spirals.storyDepth: perspective shifts, reported speech, hypothetical, the shadow self
spirals.note: 13 of 21 games recycle ALL prior LTs in argumentative/perspective contexts. 'No tengas miedo' (A2 imperative) becomes 'Don Próspero dijo que no tuviéramos miedo del progreso' (B2 reported speech + imperfect subjunctive). Same root imperative, but now filtered through another voice. 8 games introduce B2-specific targets.

spirals.storyDepth: symbolic, metaphorical, dream-logic, register shifts, ancestors speak in poetry
spirals.note: 21 of 34 games revisit ALL prior LTs in abstract/symbolic contexts. The student doesn't learn new grammar for old forms — they learn to USE old forms in new registers. Fewer new words, more interpretation. 13 games introduce C1-specific targets (imperfect subjunctive, si clauses, register adaptation).

spirals.storyDepth: authorial, the student creates language, mediates between voices, writes myth
spirals.note: 34 of 55 games revisit ALL prior LTs. The student now USES the entire grammar system to create, mediate, and resolve. The 21 new C2-specific targets are discourse-level (literary register, nuanced connectors, mediation/reformulation). Mastery is not new grammar — it's what you DO with all the grammar.

fibonacciRule.law: Each spiral has approximately as many game instances as ALL previous spirals combined
fibonacciRule.consequence: At every new level, there is ROOM to revisit every prior LT at least once, plus introduce new ones. This is spiral learning by construction, not by hope.

recyclingRule.law: A recycled LT keeps its ontology ID but gains new context from the StoryNode
mechanism: Same lt_verb_tener_indicative_present at A1: 'Tengo cinco años' (stating age)
Same LT at A2: 'Tengo miedo del bosque' (expressing fear — tener expression)
Same LT at B1: 'Yaguará tenía miedo cuando era pequeña' (narrating past — NEW tense wraps OLD target)
Same LT at B2: 'Dijo que tenía miedo, pero no le creí' (reported speech — ANOTHER voice wraps it)
Same LT at C1: 'Si tuviera miedo, no hablaría' (hypothetical — the form bends into subjunctive)
Same LT at C2: 'El miedo es la raíz de todas las puertas que no abrimos' (the concept transcends grammar)
recyclingRule.ontologyLink: The LT ID stays the same. The StoryNode provides the new context. The LearningObjective at each level provides the new can-do. Grammar is the constant; meaning is the variable.

stages.context: Yaguará finds Candelaria lost by the river at night. Candelaria is afraid. Yaguará must tell her not to be afraid — but in human words.
stages.storyDepth: Simple command. One speaker, one listener. Present moment.
stages.whatStudentLearns: The FORM: no + subjunctive present = negative command

stages.storyNode: sn_dest21 (Don Próspero's road threatens the forest)
stages.context: Don Próspero tells the community: 'No tengan miedo del progreso.' Candelaria whispers to Yaguará: 'No le tengas confianza.' Two negative commands — one public, one private. Who is right?
stages.storyDepth: The SAME form now carries social weight. Two people use the same grammar for opposite purposes. The student sees: language is power.
stages.culturalConcept: cc_escuchadora (Doña Asunción warned about men like Próspero)
stages.whatStudentLearns: The CONTEXT: same form, but WHO gives the command and WHY changes everything

stages.context: La Sombra speaks in confused grammar: 'No tienes miedo... no ten miedo... no...' The student must recognize which forms are correct and which are the Shadow's confusion. Then write the correct command to dispel the Shadow.
stages.prompt: La Sombra dice: 'No tienes miedo del silencio.' ¿Es correcto? Escribe la forma correcta.
stages.storyDepth: The Shadow is the student's own fear of making mistakes. To correct the Shadow, you must KNOW the form AND have the courage to speak it. The grammar becomes psychological.
stages.whatStudentLearns: The MASTERY: the student corrects broken language. They are now the authority. Same form, but the student's relationship to it has inverted — from receiver to speaker to teacher.

workedExample.summary: 3 Fibonacci stages. Same ontology ID (lt_grammar_imperative_negative). Same grammatical form (no tengas). Three completely different experiences. The grammar didn't change. The student did.
